summary: External-review improvement cycle (response to berendt I-001/I-002). Resolved via Option A (docs-only fix): tightened the docblock for pushSecretToKeystoneMapper (operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:835-839) and the corresponding 'Name match' / 'Namespace scope' rows in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md to acknowledge that obj.GetNamespace() being non-empty is an apiserver invariant for the namespaced PushSecret resource, rather than a behaviour the mapper itself enforces. Renamed TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_EmptyNamespaceReturnsNil to TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_NoKeystonesInNamespaceReturnsNil to match what the body actually exercises. Added a new review pattern file (verify-framework-helper-semantics-for-zero-values.md) and updated update-documentation-when-referenced-code-behavior-changes.md to capture the lesson. All BLOCKER criteria continue to hold: a single Watches() registration with mapper + predicate, no Owns() residue, namespace-scoped List, never returns an error, predicate suppresses status-only updates, no edits to reconcile_secrets.go or the integration tests. All TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_* and TestPushSecretPredicate_* unit tests pass.

tests_checklist.item: All TestPushSecretToKeystoneMapper_* and TestPushSecretPredicate_* tests pass
Renamed test still asserts the documented behaviour (no Keystones in non-empty namespace returns nil)
No assertion changes regress the existing mapper/predicate coverage
reconcile_secrets_test.go and the three TestIntegration_* tests are untouched

code_quality_checklist.item: Docblock in keystone_controller.go now matches what the code enforces (no overstated guarantee)
Docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md table rows mirror the in-source docblock and avoid duplicating the apiserver-invariant claim inconsistently
Test function name describes the path the body actually exercises
No dead code, no unused imports, no formatting drift

security_checklist.item: No new attack surface introduced by docs/test-name changes
Namespace-scoping invariant remains load-bearing and is documented as such

architecture_checklist.item: Single Watches() registration for esov1alpha1.PushSecret with mapper + predicate; no Owns() residue
No code-side guard added — Option A keeps the mapper symmetrically minimal vs the other *ToKeystoneMapper helpers
No new field indexer; in-memory compare against the 2-element openBaoBackupPushSecretNames is preserved

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No speculative empty-namespace guard added; relies on the apiserver invariant the docs now describe
Doc updates touch only the two rows that overstated the guarantee — no cosmetic churn

fail_fast_checklist.item: List error path remains: log via log.FromContext, return nil — handler.MapFunc contract preserved

defensive_checklist.item: Namespace-scoping is enforced by the unconditional client.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()); cross-namespace test (recordingListInterceptor) still pins this
Status-only update suppression continues to be exercised by TestPushSecretPredicate_StatusOnlyUpdateSuppressed

issues_found.description: The new CC-0092 example was appended to .planwerk/review_patterns/test-script-timeout-must-exceed-worst-case-loop-duration.md, but that pattern is about retry/polling loops with sleep exceeding a framework-level script timeout. The CC-0092 fix is about a misleading test-function name (TestX_EmptyNamespaceReturnsNil that does not exercise the empty-namespace path). The semantically matching pattern already exists at .planwerk/review_patterns/ensure-test-names-accurately-describe-their-coverage-scope.md (severity WARNING, occurrences 6) and contains identical-shape examples (CC-0011, CC-0072, etc.). Misclassification dilutes the timeout pattern's signal and hides the example from future reviewers searching the test-naming pattern.
issues_found.fix: Move the CC-0092 example block (and the +1 occurrences bump) from test-script-timeout-must-exceed-worst-case-loop-duration.md into ensure-test-names-accurately-describe-their-coverage-scope.md. Decrement Occurrences in the timeout file to 2 and increment Occurrences in the test-names file to 7.
issues_found.evidence: test-script-timeout-must-exceed-worst-case-loop-duration.md:34 — '**What was missed**: Test function names like TestX_EmptyNamespaceReturnsNil should actually exercise the empty-namespace path...' — this hint is about test naming, not loop-duration vs script-timeout arithmetic.

suggested_improvements: Optional cosmetic: the docblock at keystone_controller.go:840-842 still wraps 'matching the behaviour of' onto its own line before listing the sibling mappers. The wrap is a holdover from the pre-edit text; reflowing the paragraph would tighten readability but is not load-bearing.
next_steps: Move the CC-0092 entry in test-script-timeout-must-exceed-worst-case-loop-duration.md to ensure-test-names-accurately-describe-their-coverage-scope.md and adjust both Occurrences counters.
